notes.anchor: when as quaestor you refused to obey a tribune of the plebs
notes.note: Trebonius was quaestor in 60 BC; Cicero recalls his stand on the optimate side, refusing a tribune's order even when his fellow quaestor complied. The episode is otherwise unrecorded.

notes.anchor: had you not been suddenly dispatched to Spain
notes.note: Trebonius was sent as Caesar's legate to Further Spain in 47 BC to deal with the unrest there; the abrupt posting cut short his plan to meet the returning Cicero at Brundisium.

notes.anchor: I sent that letter to Calvus
notes.note: C. Licinius Macer Calvus, orator and neoteric poet (d. c. 47 BC) and leader of the Atticist movement; Cicero refers to a now-lost letter in which he had praised Calvus's talent, part of their debate over oratorical style.

notes.anchor: we write in one way what we mean only for those to whom we send it
notes.note: Cicero distinguishes the private letter from the published one: he had praised Calvus warmly in a private note never meant to circulate, and would not have done so for a wider readership.
